risks.title: Stale semantic headers degrade RAG quality
risks.mitigation: Run populate-coderef --overwrite-headers before RAG index rebuild to reduce stale=1112

risks.title: Layer constraint broken — rag-search --constraint layer always returns 0
risks.mitigation: Use --constraint type:<x> instead of layer for filtered queries; file stub for layer propagation fix

risks.title: 0 capability/layer in element metadata — semantic headers exist but not parsed
risks.mitigation: After overwrite-headers pass, re-run coderef-scan to populate metadata fields; verify capability/layer counts > 0 before RAG query phase

risks.title: RAG index only 1156 records vs 2431 elements — low coverage ratio
risks.mitigation: Rebuild RAG index after header refresh to increase vector coverage

self_scan_query_design.rationale: These RAG queries are designed to surface self-improvement signal from CODEREF-CORE's own index. Each targets a distinct gap category.

queries: module without capability or layer metadata
query_categories.expected_signal: Files/elements with defined=false or stale headers; modules that lack @layer or @capability
Central orchestration files, high-element-count modules, import chains between scanner and indexer
High-complexity, low-coverage files surfaced by hotspot analysis
Elements with headerStatus=stale or defined but capability=null

decision_records.decision: Use --provider ollama for all RAG operations — no OpenAI key available
decision_records.rationale: Ollama nomic-embed-text already indexed and active (1156 records). OpenAI would require API key and incur cost.
1112 stale headers means semantic content is old. Fresh headers → better embeddings → better self-scan signal.

decision_records.decision: Self-scan produces a markdown report, not a new workorder
decision_records.rationale: Output is human-readable signal for the operator to review. A structured WO creation step follows only if actionable items are found.
